verdict_reasoning: HMGB1 is primarily a nuclear chromatin-binding protein that also has documented cytoplasmic and extracellular roles. Under inflammatory activation, oxidative stress, necrosis, or pyroptosis, HMGB1 is actively secreted or passively released and can bind wash-resistantly to the cell surface via interaction with surface receptors (RAGE, TLR4, TLR2, syndecan-1) on neighboring or the same cells. Multiple flow cytometry and surface proteomics studies on intact non-permeabilized immune and tumor cells confirm surface HMGB1 under these conditions. Therapeutic antibodies and anti-HMGB1 programs targeting its extracellular/surface form exist. Cell-state-induced: yes—secretion/release requires activation or cell death stress. Tissue-restricted: no—broad cell types. Lysosomal exocytosis: not the primary mechanism. Dual localization: nuclear dominant but surface pool documented. Stable surface attachment: binds surface RAGE/TLRs; wash-resistance is partial. Overall best fit is cell_state_induced surface display.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Degree of wash-resistant surface anchoring vs. soluble secreted pool is debated; surface display may be transient receptor-binding rather than stable co-trafficking.
